name: Card Flyby · accelerating 3D deck cascade
one_line: A depth column of N cards rolls forward through perspective: on each landing beat the next card tumbles up into the front slot and WIPES in its solid colored face (angled clip-path), the previous front card tumbles forward + falls toward the camera on exit, and the cards behind advance one slot — dwells shrink card-to-card so the deck reads as an accelerating rush into a held final card.

base_template.notes: A 3D deck of N cards stacked in a perspective depth column (slots s0=front … s3 + far=back, plus an exit pose). Each card is a CSS wireframe (always present, opacity tracks its slot) over a solid face — a gentle top→bottom tonal gradient (--c1/--c2) — revealed by an angled clip-path wipe (--wipe 0→1). The scene carries a static yaw; the cards' own rotateX drives the tumble. N is DATA (the `cards` array) — the slot engine is rel-based (a card's pose is a pure function of how many slots it sits behind the current front card), so 3 / 6 / 9 cards all 'just work'. COLOR is a `theme` (a cohesive palette ramp cycled across the deck), per-card overridable. Asset-free: wireframe + solid cards + system type, bundled GSAP.

themes._: Cohesive palette presets (the `theme` param). Each is ONE temperature/saturation family so any card count reads as a designed set — the deck cycles the ramp in order. White-on-card stays AA at the card type sizes. Per-card `color` still overrides.
themes.aurora: cool jewel arc — teal → ocean → indigo → violet → plum, on blue-black (DEFAULT)
themes.ember: warm sunset arc — wine → brick → rust → amber → ochre → bronze, on warm near-black
themes.mono: sleek monochrome blue staircase (dark → mid), on blue-black
themes.slate: muted cool slate-grays — understated / editorial, on cool charcoal

match.notes: Reach for this when a section has a STREAM of discrete onsets that ACCELERATE (gaps shrinking / a build into a downbeat) and you want to flash a SEQUENCE of items — a deck of titles, projects, posters, video tiles — one per hit, climaxing on a held card. Each card lands on one onset (per_onset_sequential), so it shares the structural_type with the typewriter / logo-sting templates; it is discriminated by being a SEQUENCE OF WHOLE CARDS (not letters/words of one message) and by the accelerating cadence. The strongest hit (final downbeat / surge) should carry the card you want held longest. Candidate finer type: an `accel-roll` flavor of `roll_cascade` (the shrinking-dwell build) — flagged for catalog review, not yet a vocab value. If the grid goes quiet (onset_gap_bars >= ~1.5) this is the wrong template — use a hold template.

examples.cadence: accelerating (gaps shrink ~0.78× each card); final card holds ~1.6s
examples.note: NOT reversed from a golden sample. This template was authored from the `card-flyby` SANDBOX composition (a Shader-Deck-style flyby), so these audio facts are the DESIGN-INTENT cadence (the hand-tuned landing schedule), not values derived from an `audiomap.json`. When this template is matched to a real group, `landings` is bound from that group's onset times (one per card) and the schedule re-derives — see beat_map.

deck_card.notes: Each of the N cards. On every landing beat the whole column advances one slot toward the front (far→s3→s2→s1→s0). A card's pose is a pure function of its slot-distance behind the front card (rel), so the deck stays correctly depth-sorted for any N. PROPOSED verb 'deck_advance' (per-onset one-slot forward roll of a stacked depth column; ~content_swap promoted to a 3D column).

front_face.notes: As the landing card rolls into s0 (upright, rotX 0), its SOLID colored face — a big CENTERED hero title + a small play glyph — is revealed by an angled clip-path wipe sweeping upper-left→lower-right (--wipe 0→1, ~0.72° diagonal). The title is the only copy on the face (no eyebrow tag / no meta footer). ~05-mask_reveal.

exit_card.notes: When a new card lands, the previous front card continues FORWARD past upright into a face-down tumble (rotX → -88°) while dropping DOWN and toward the camera (y +620, z +420, scale 1.10) and fading out. PROPOSED verb 'tumble_fall' (forward over-rotate + fall toward lens). Reverse of the incoming roll.

field.notes: A static radial-gradient field behind the deck (default near-black #06070d). Dead still the whole span; the only motion is the deck. negative_space_hold.

theme.notes: Palette preset: aurora | ember | mono | slate. Sets the field bg + the cohesive card-color ramp the deck cycles + the ink. The fastest knob for a whole new look.

bgColor.notes: Override the field background. Empty = the theme's bg. The radial centre/edge are derived from it.

cards.notes: JSON array of card objects { title, color? }. `title` is the big centered hero (the only required field). Its length sets N (the deck depth). `color` is OPTIONAL — omit it and the card takes the next color from the theme ramp (cohesive by default); set it to override one card. Empty = the built-in 6-card demo deck. THIS is the compose-by-data surface for content: add / drop / reorder cards — no code edit.

landings.notes: JSON array of absolute audio seconds — ONE landing beat per card (length must equal `cards` length). Empty = an accelerating schedule auto-derived from the card count + duration. Bind these from the matched group's onset times so each card lands on a hit.

yaw.notes: Static side-tilt of the whole deck. 0 = face-on; negative = scene rotated so the deck reads from the left.

beat_map.comment: Musical TIMING lives here; content lives in `cards`. All times are ABSOLUTE audio seconds. One landing per card; per-card transition duration is derived from the gap into each landing (faster as the cadence accelerates).
beat_map.landings_notes: one per card, in deck order. Bind from the matched group's onset times. The schedule should ACCELERATE (gaps shrinking) for the signature build; the final card lands early enough to HOLD a beat before the cut. If omitted at build time, an accelerating schedule is generated from card count + duration.
beat_map.transition_dur_rule: dur_i = clamp(0.32 * gap_into_i, 0.12, 0.42); each landing tween starts at landing - dur (leads the beat) so the card arrives ON the hit; the face wipe runs over the last ~85% of that window.
beat_map.exit_note: the final card rolls in and HOLDS front-and-center to the end (6.0s); the source then hard-cuts to the next group (fast_cut).

engine.model: data deck + rel-based slot column. The deck (`cards`) and the landing beats (`landings`) are DATA; the slot poses (s0..far + exit) and the per-landing orchestration are fixed code. `poseForRel(rel)` maps a card's slot-distance behind the current front card to a pose, so the column is always correctly depth-sorted and any N works. z-index is set instantly at each swap (CSS can't depth-sort non-intersecting planes). Compose by editing the `cards` / `landings` DATA — never the slot/orchestration code.
engine.extend: recolor the whole deck via `theme` (cohesive ramp) or one card via its `color`; more cards = more `cards` entries (+ matching `landings`); change the read angle via `yaw`. Add a palette = add an entry to the THEMES table (bg + ink + a colors[] ramp). To change the deck LOOK (slot depths, card size, wipe angle) edit the SLOT table / CSS, not the program.

program_format._: `cards` is a JSON array of card objects; `landings` is a JSON array of absolute audio seconds, one per card (same length as `cards`).

card.title: string — the big CENTERED hero title (the only copy on the face)
card.color: string? — OPTIONAL solid face color (CSS color). Omit = next color from the theme ramp.

program_format.landings: number[] — abs seconds, one landing beat per card, in deck order (accelerating cadence recommended)

program_data: program.json (the default 6-card deck + landing schedule as standalone, agent-editable data)
uses_primitives_notes: deck_advance / wipe_reveal / tumble_fall are PROPOSED custom phrases the impl defines (a per-onset one-slot forward roll of a stacked 3D depth column; an angled clip-path face reveal; a forward over-rotate + fall toward the lens). 18-3d_card_flip (#18) backs the 3D card transforms; 05-mask_reveal (#05) backs the angled clip-path wipe; 09-dolly_zoom (#09) is the perspective/depth feel; content_swap is the per-onset advance generalized to a depth column; negative_space_hold backs the static field.
bundled_assets: assets/gsap.min.js (offline GSAP). No fonts bundled — uses a system grotesque stack ('Inter', 'Helvetica Neue', Arial). No remote assets.

provenance.note: Hand-authored design study, NOT reverse-engineered from a golden-sample group — there is no source audiomap / Gemini read. The match-face is therefore a HYPOTHESIS (which music this look fits), not a measured signature; `examples[].synthetic = true` flags the cadence as design-intent. The original sandbox is asset-free but used remote Google-Fonts Inter + a CDN GSAP AND a grab-bag of clashing per-card hexes; this promoted impl drops the remote deps (system fonts + bundled GSAP), makes the deck data-driven (`cards` / `landings`), and replaces the ad-hoc colors with a cohesive `theme` palette system (per-card color optional). Promote-time review should add a REAL example (and tighten `requires`) the first time this template is matched to an actual reversed group.
